LAYER_ORDER.description: Stateful services with own healthchecks (Postgres, Redis, Kafka, vector DBs, Temporal).
Schema migrations + initial DDL on the infrastructure layer (alembic upgrade head, prisma migrate deploy).
Data-shape provisioning (vector collections, kafka topics, redis streams). Reads bootstrap_inputs from upstream caps.
User / tenant / service-account provisioning. Empty in MVP recipes; reserved for auth provider bootstrap.
Tracing + log-aggregation backends (Langfuse, Langsmith, Grafana stack). Often `requires: [relational]`.
Eval harnesses (promptfoo, deepeval, ragas) — config + golden datasets prepped before the agent boots.
The agent process itself. Last to boot; first to be smoke-tested.
User-facing UI / chat surface. Optional; only present when the recipe declares a frontend capability.

patterns.name: Agentic RAG
patterns.description: RAG where the agent plans retrievals, decomposes queries, routes across sources, reflects on sufficiency, and enforces citation-bound answers.

patterns.name: Evaluator-Optimizer
patterns.description: Generate-evaluate feedback loop that iteratively improves output.

patterns.name: Event-Driven
patterns.description: Agents triggered by queue or stream events rather than HTTP requests.

patterns.name: Long-Horizon
patterns.description: Multi-session agent tasks that span hours to weeks; checkpoint-and-resume across crashes, deploys, and external waits.

patterns.name: Multi-Agent
patterns.description: Supervisor-worker delegation across multiple autonomous agents.

patterns.name: Orchestrator-Worker
patterns.description: LLM decomposes a task and delegates to specialized workers.

patterns.name: Parallel Calls
patterns.description: Concurrent LLM calls on independent inputs, aggregated at the end.

patterns.name: Plan & Execute
patterns.description: LLM creates a full plan upfront, then executes each step sequentially.

patterns.name: Prompt Chaining
patterns.description: Sequential LLM calls with validation gates between steps.

patterns.name: RAG
patterns.description: Retrieval-augmented generation: retrieve relevant context before generating.

patterns.name: ReAct
patterns.description: Reason-act loop: the LLM reasons, calls a tool, observes, and repeats until done.

patterns.name: Reflection
patterns.description: LLM critiques its own output and self-improves through structured feedback.

patterns.name: Routing
patterns.description: Intent classification dispatches inputs to specialized handlers.

patterns.name: Saga
patterns.description: Long-running, multi-step business processes that need compensation when an intermediate step fails.

workflows.name: Evaluator-Optimizer
workflows.description: Generate-evaluate feedback loop that iteratively improves output.

workflows.name: Orchestrator-Worker
workflows.description: LLM decomposes a task and delegates to specialized workers.

workflows.name: Parallel Calls
workflows.description: Concurrent LLM calls on independent inputs, aggregated at the end.

workflows.name: Prompt Chaining
workflows.description: Sequential LLM calls with validation gates between steps.

primitives.name: Memory
primitives.description: Persistent state across sessions: short-term, long-term, and semantic memory.

primitives.name: Skills
primitives.description: File-based, agent-discovered procedural modules. Cheap to ship many; loaded on demand at runtime.

primitives.name: Sub-agents
primitives.description: Named, role-scoped agent instances spawned by a parent for delimited tasks; each has its own context window, tool grants, and (optionally) model.

primitives.name: Tool Use
primitives.description: Structured function calling with schema-validated tool dispatch.

modifiers.name: Guardrails
modifiers.description: Layered input / tool / output policy checks plus a dual-LLM split that breaks the indirect-prompt-injection path.

modifiers.name: Human in the Loop
modifiers.description: Agent proposes an action; a human approves, denies, or modifies before the action commits.

compositions.rationale: Chain is the simplest form of worker

recipes.title: Recipe: Claude Code Subagent
recipes.agent_role: You are a focused coding subagent. Execute the delegated task precisely using the available tools, and report a concise result.

roles.name: researcher
roles.description: Subagent that takes a research question and returns a list of cited findings.

default.description: Anthropic Claude via Claude Agent SDK — Sonnet for host, Sonnet for subagents.

smoke_test.exercise:
curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:8000/ask \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"question":"what files are in this repo?"}'

recipes.title: Recipe: Code Review Agent
recipes.agent_role: You are a code review assistant. Review the provided changes for correctness, security, and style; cite specific lines and suggest concrete fixes.

default.description: Anthropic Claude + E2B sandbox for running test repros.

local_only.description: Self-hosted vLLM. Sandbox stays on E2B (no fully-local alternative yet).

recipes.title: Recipe: Content Pipeline
recipes.agent_role: You are a content production assistant. Turn the user's brief into polished, on-brand content through draft, edit, and finalize stages.

default.description: Anthropic Claude Sonnet across all chain steps.

local_only.description: Self-hosted vLLM serving Llama 3 70B for content steps.

recipes.title: Recipe: Customer Support Triage
recipes.agent_role: You are a customer support agent. Understand the user's issue, route it to the right specialty, and respond clearly and empathetically.

roles.name: classifier
roles.description: Read incoming user message, classify intent into {billing, technical, account, general} with confidence + reasoning.

roles.name: billing-specialist
roles.description: Answer billing questions; look up customer subscription state in Stripe; refund/credit decisions.

roles.name: technical-specialist
roles.description: Answer technical/product questions; search the knowledge base; surface diagnostic steps.

roles.name: account-specialist
roles.description: Account-management questions (password, profile, plan changes); look up account state.

default.description: Anthropic Claude — Haiku for classification, Sonnet for specialists.

smoke_test.exercise:
curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:8000/support \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"message":"I have a billing question"}'

recipes.title: Recipe: docs-rag-qa
recipes.agent_role: You are a documentation assistant. Answer using only the retrieved documents and cite each source; if the docs do not cover it, say so plainly.

default.description: Anthropic + OpenAI embeddings + Cohere rerank + Qdrant + Langfuse.

local_only.description: Self-hosted vLLM + BGE embeddings + BGE-reranker, no SaaS keys.

smoke_test.exercise:
curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:8000/ask \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"question":"What is the canonical stack?"}'

recipes.title: Recipe: Hierarchical Agent
recipes.agent_role: You are the lead of a hierarchical agent team. Break the goal into subtasks, delegate to workers, and synthesize their results into a final answer.

roles.name: supervisor
roles.description: Coordinate the team by routing tasks to specialists and synthesizing their outputs.

roles.name: researcher
roles.description: Find information, extract facts, gather source material.

roles.name: writer
roles.description: Draft and revise content based on the research and any feedback.

roles.name: reviewer
roles.description: Evaluate content for accuracy, completeness, clarity, and style.

default.description: Anthropic Claude — Opus for supervisor, Sonnet for specialists.

local_only.description: Self-hosted vLLM serving Llama 3 70B for all roles.

smoke_test.exercise:
curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:8000/task \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"task":"write a one-paragraph explainer about Postgres MVCC"}'

recipes.title: Recipe: Memory Assistant
recipes.agent_role: You are a personal assistant with long-term memory. Recall relevant prior context, help the user, and remember new facts they share.

default.description: Anthropic Claude + Zep for memory, OpenAI for embeddings.

local_only.description: Self-hosted vLLM + Zep (OSS image). Embeddings stay on OpenAI for now.

smoke_test.exercise:
curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:8000/chat \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"user_id":"smoke","message":"remember that my favorite color is blue"}'

recipes.title: Recipe: Ops Crew
recipes.agent_role: You are an operations assistant. Triage the incident, follow the runbook, and produce a clear incident summary with concrete next steps.

roles.name: devops
roles.description: Diagnose infrastructure issues and recommend operational fixes.

roles.name: security
roles.description: Assess security posture; identify vulnerabilities and suspicious activity.

roles.name: database
roles.description: Analyze database performance and schema health.

default.description: Anthropic Claude — Sonnet for each specialist.

local_only.description: Self-hosted vLLM serving Llama 3 70B for all specialists.

smoke_test.exercise:
curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:8000/investigate \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"incident":"smoke test - DB connection pool saturated"}'

recipes.title: Recipe: Parallel Enricher
recipes.agent_role: You are a data enrichment assistant. Enrich each input record by gathering attributes in parallel and returning a consolidated result.

default.description: Anthropic Claude Haiku for the enrichment fan-out.

recipes.title: Recipe: Research Assistant
recipes.agent_role: You are a research assistant. Plan the investigation, use tools to gather evidence, and deliver a well-sourced, structured answer.

default.description: Anthropic Claude + Tavily web search + local Postgres/Redis/Langfuse.

local_only.description: Self-hosted vLLM (no Anthropic key needed) + SearXNG search.

recipes.title: Recipe: Restaurant Rebooking
recipes.agent_role: You are a restaurant rebooking assistant. When a reservation is disrupted, find suitable alternatives and confirm a new booking with the user.

roles.name: intake
roles.description: Consume reservation-change events from Redis Streams, classify (cancel / no-show / modify), build the case envelope.

roles.name: eligibility
roles.description: Apply auto-rebook policy rules (tier, time window, customer history) to dispatch the case along one of three terminal paths (rebook / notify host only / decline).

roles.name: search
roles.description: Query alternative slots across reservation platforms (Resy, OpenTable, Toast) and rank candidates.

roles.name: notifier
roles.description: Compose and send rebooking offers via email/SMS; record customer acceptance back to the case.

default.description: Anthropic Claude + Redis Streams + Postgres + Langfuse.

hybrid_kafka.description: Same as default but Kafka instead of Redis Streams for >10k events/sec.

smoke_test.exercise:
# Publish a synthetic cancellation event onto the input stream
docker compose exec -T redis redis-cli XADD reservations.cancelled '*' \
  reservation_id smoke-1 customer_id smoke party_size 2 cancel_window_minutes 60
# Wait briefly for the worker to process
sleep 2
# Read back any rebooked-event the worker emitted
docker compose exec -T redis redis-cli XLEN reservations.rebooked

card.name: Runtime API-key bootstrap
card.description: Lets the chat UI capture the agent's Anthropic API key at runtime via a CSRF-guarded /setup form on the backend, when it wasn't pre-wired — so a freshly-cloned project can `docker compose up` and start chatting after pasting a key once. Dev sandbox only.

capabilities.when_to_load: a chat frontend is present and the agent needs a key to reply

card.name: Redis
card.description: Redis 7 in-memory key-value store with Streams, hashes, and pub/sub.

card.name: Temporal OSS
card.description: Durable-execution workflow engine for long-horizon agent tasks with checkpoint-resume, signals, and visibility.

card.name: OpenAI Embeddings
card.description: OpenAI text-embedding-3-small (1536 dim) for RAG ingestion and query encoding.

card.name: DeepEval
card.description: Python-native LLM/RAG eval framework with pytest integration and built-in RAG metrics.

card.name: Promptfoo
card.description: YAML-first LLM evaluation harness with provider matrix, LLM-judge grading, and CI gating.

card.name: RAGAS
card.description: Academic-style RAG benchmarking against a labeled dataset with answer/context metrics.

card.name: Minimal Chat UI (Vite + React)
card.description: A tiny Vite + React + TypeScript chat that POSTs to the backend and renders the reply. Built once and served from nginx as a container — the default frontend for every agent.

card.name: Next.js Chat UI
card.description: Next.js 14 (App Router) chat template wired for Vercel AI SDK streaming responses.

card.name: Streamlit Chat UI
card.description: Single-file Streamlit chat template for Python-only stacks.

card.name: Llama Guard 3 (via Together AI)
card.description: Meta's Llama Guard 3 (8B) safety classifier for input/output policy enforcement and jailbreak detection.

card.name: Fly.io
card.description: Container-hosted backend with per-region rollout and Fly Machines API for fine-grained scaling.

card.name: Railway
card.description: Container-hosted backend with managed Postgres + Redis from one platform.

card.name: Vercel
card.description: Edge-hosted serverless deploy target with native Next.js + streaming-response support.

card.name: Tavily (Direct API)
card.description: Tavily web search via direct REST API — same vendor as mcp.tavily without MCP framing.

card.name: Tavily (MCP)
card.description: Tavily web search exposed as an MCP server over streamable_http transport.

capabilities.when_to_load: recipe declares mcp_servers with capability: mcp.tavily

card.name: Zep
card.description: Long-term agent memory store with conversation summarization, session/user scoping, and semantic recall.

card.name: Grafana stack (Grafana + Prometheus + Tempo)
card.description: Metrics + traces + dashboards for the full agent stack via OpenTelemetry.

card.name: Langfuse
card.description: Self-hosted LLM observability with traces, scores, and dataset-backed evaluations.

card.name: LangSmith
card.description: Hosted LLM observability for LangChain / LangGraph apps. Auto-instruments via env vars.

card.name: Apache Kafka
card.description: Kafka 3.x in KRaft mode (no ZooKeeper) for high-throughput durable event log.

card.name: Redis Streams
card.description: Redis Streams as the event source — log-shaped data type with consumer groups and ACK.

card.name: PostgreSQL
card.description: Postgres 16 with JSONB, transactions, and pgvector extension support.

card.name: Cohere Rerank v3.5
card.description: Late-stage rerank step that re-orders retrieval results by relevance to the query.

card.name: E2B Code Interpreter
card.description: Hosted sandbox running LLM-emitted code in an isolated container with file artifacts.

card.name: Chroma
card.description: Open-source vector DB with single-container deploy and HTTP-only API.

card.name: pgvector
card.description: Postgres extension adding a `vector` data type with cosine/euclidean/dot-product distance operators.

card.name: Qdrant
card.description: Self-hosted vector DB with REST + gRPC endpoints, named collections, and payload filtering.

versions.notes: 0.2.x is the current line. API surface still evolving — pin tight and re-verify on every minor bump.
0.3.x is the current line. API surface still evolving — pin tight and re-verify on every minor bump. Semver runs independently from the Python package.
>=0.70.0 is the floor at which the kwargs-only `Crew()` constructor + the split-out memory module are stable; older drops break in unobvious ways.
0.3.x consolidated agents under langchain.agents; earlier versions split between langchain and langchain-experimental. >=0.4 may break the tool decorator surface — pin tight.
0.3.x is the recipe-validated floor; pin tight because the checkpointer + prebuilt agent surface still shifts between minors.
Pre-1.0 line; the ^0.1.0 floor unlocks the agents + workflows + memory triad recipes assume, but the surface still moves between minors. Pin tight.
The >=0.1.0 floor is what stabilizes the agent + `output_type` (renamed from `result_type`) surface the recipes rely on; treat the 0.0.x line as legacy.
The ^4.0.0 major rewrote `streamText` / `generateText` / `generateObject` and is the recipe-validated line; 3.x is incompatible, 5.x untested.

stack.when_to_load: recipe declares relational.postgres and high concurrency

suggestions.description: Per-combo stack recommendations scoped to upstream blueprints version dfd824d. One file per pattern × primitives × modifiers combination.
